description: Test cases for iOS view controller hierarchy inspection

evals.prompt: Determine the current view controller screen name in an iOS app.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui.inspect to get navigationBar.title
2. Title typically corresponds to current view controller
3. Example: 'Settings' indicates SettingsViewController
4. If no navigation bar, may need alternative detection

evals.prompt: Check if the iOS app is at the root controller or in a navigation stack.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui.inspect to check navigationBar.backAvailable
2. If true: in navigation stack (depth >= 2)
3. If false: at root controller (depth = 1)
4. Can use this to determine if back navigation is possible

evals.prompt: Get the complete view controller hierarchy of an iOS app using ui.controllers command.
evals.expected_output:
⚠️ NOT TESTED YET
Expected behavior:
1. Call ui.controllers (no parameters)
2. Should return root controller type
3. Should return topmost visible controller
4. Should return navigation stack array
5. Should indicate modal presentations
6. Requires testing before production use
